items.title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
items.summary: Someone’s offering an unusual deal for a 13-acre property in Mill Valley, just north of South Francisco.

items.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
items.summary: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

items.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
items.summary: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

items.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
items.summary: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

items.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
items.summary: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

items.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
items.summary: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

items.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
items.summary: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

items.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
items.summary: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

items.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
items.summary: Hundreds of subdomains from dozens of universities have been hijacked by scammers.

items.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
items.summary: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

items.title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B
items.summary: A new era is on the way for Apple as Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus.   Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different eco

items.title: Marked-up Mac minis flood eBay amid shortages driven by AI
items.summary: Apple’s sold-out Mac mini is spawning marked-up eBay listings as demand surges for the compact desktop, now favored for running local AI models and tools.

items.title: Nothing introduces an AI-powered dictation tool
items.summary: Nothing's new on-device dictation tool supports over 100 languages.

items.title: DeepSeek previews new AI model that ‘closes the gap’ with frontier models
items.summary: DeepSeek says both models are more efficient and performant than DeepSeek V3.2 due to architectural improvements, and have almost "closed the gap" with current leading models, both open and closed, on reasoning benchmarks.

items.title: In another wild turn for AI chips, Meta signs deal for millions of Amazon AI CPUs
items.summary: Meta has commandeered a big chunk of Amazon's homegrown CPUs (not GPUs) for AI agentic workloads, signaling that a new kind of chip race has begun.

items.title: Bret Taylor’s Sierra buys YC-backed AI startup Fragment
items.summary: Sierra, the AI customer service agent startup founded by technologist Bret Taylor, announced today that it has acquired the YC-backed French startup Fragment.

items.title: Meet Noscroll, an AI bot that does your doomscrolling for you
items.summary: Noscroll wants to cure doomscrolling with an AI bot that reads the internet for you.

items.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5, bringing company one step closer to an AI ‘super app’
items.summary: OpenAI says its latest model offers increased capabilities across a broad variety of categories.

items.title: Microsoft issues emergency update for macOS and Linux ASP.NET threat
items.summary: When authentication fails, things can go very, very wrong.

items.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
items.summary: Grinex says needed hacking resources "available exclusively to... unfriendly states."

items.title: Executive Briefing: The AI cost curve your strategy is riding just broke + 3 prompts to find your exposure
items.summary: Watch now | The inference economics nobody is pricing in — plus the prompts to audit your own exposure.

items.title: What GPT-Image-2 actually changed — and the creative ops function that makes you the one who compounds from it
items.summary: Watch now | OpenAI shipped an image model this week that plans before it draws, searches the web while it composes, and checks its own work before returning the result.

items.title: Claude Design just cut 60% of your designer's week — here's what to do with the rest + 4 prompts
items.summary: Watch now | How Claude Design, Claude Code, and Cowork together quietly retire the entire mockup-to-production handoff.

items.title: DeepSeek V4 - almost on the frontier, a fraction of the price
items.summary: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek's last model release was V3.2 (and V3.2 Speciale) last December. They just dropped the first of their hotly anticipated V4 series in the shape of two preview models, DeepSeek-V4-Pro and DeepSeek-V4-Flash. Both models are 1 million token context Mixture of Experts. Pro is 1.6T

items.title: Extract PDF text in your browser with LiteParse for the web
items.summary: LlamaIndex have a most excellent open source project called LiteParse, which provides a Node.js CLI tool for extracting text from PDFs. I got a version of LiteParse working entirely in the browser, using most of the same libraries that LiteParse uses to run in Node.js. Spatial text parsing Refreshin

items.title: A pelican for GPT-5.5 via the semi-official Codex backdoor API
items.summary: GPT-5.5 is out. It's available in OpenAI Codex and is rolling out to paid ChatGPT subscribers. I've had some preview access and found it to be a fast, effective and highly capable model. items.title: Your automation strategy has a blind spot the size of your entire legacy stack. Codex just filled it.
items.summary: Watch now | Codex is coming for all of work — without waiting for anyone to build for agents.

items.title: Introducing GPT-5.5
items.summary: Introducing GPT-5.5, our smartest model yet—faster, more capable, and built for complex tasks like coding, research, and data analysis across tools.

items.title: GPT-5.5 System Card

items.summary: Learn how to automate tasks in Codex using schedules and triggers to create reports, summaries, and recurring workflows without manual effort.
